items.title: Content creation products
items.description: Chat assistants, marketing copy tools, and image generators are Gen AI because the delivered value is newly synthesized content, not a single classification.

items.title: Developer copilots
items.description: Code completion and refactoring suggestions generate new source text conditioned on context—generative deployment of language models, distinct from static lint rules.

items.title: Data augmentation (when sampling new examples)
items.description: Synthetic tabular rows, paraphrases, or images used to expand training sets use generative models to produce additional samples; the augmentation step is generative even if downstream training is discriminative.

items.title: Multimodal assistants
items.description: Systems that produce captions, speech, or images from mixed inputs sit in Gen AI when generation is the user-facing outcome; perception-only classifiers remain discriminative.
